title: Simple multi-item (provided example 01)
turns: Hi, can I get two birria tacos and a Mexican Coke?
notes: Public test case 01. Subtotal: 2×4.99 + 3.49 = 13.47

title: Order with modifier (provided example 02)
turns: I'll have a chicken bowl with extra guacamole, please.
notes: Public test case 02. bowl_pollo $12.49 + add_guac $1.50 = $13.99

title: Ambiguous request — must clarify (provided example 03)
notes: Must ask which burrito (birria, california, veggie, build your own). Must NOT silently pick one.

title: Single item — flan
notes: Unambiguous. Should confirm without unnecessary questions.

title: Single item — chips and guac
notes: Should resolve to side_chips_guac. No clarification needed.

title: Three distinct items
turns: I'd like a pollo bowl, chips and salsa, and a horchata

title: Three of the same item
notes: 3 × $4.49 = $13.47. Quantity must be 3, not 1 or 2.

title: Modifier: no cilantro
notes: no_cilantro modifier is $0.00. Subtotal unchanged.

title: Option: habanero salsa
turns: I'll take a veggie taco with habanero salsa
notes: Option does not affect price. Should store salsa=habanero in options.

title: Multiple modifiers on burrito
turns: Carne asada burrito with extra meat and sour cream
notes: $13.49 + extra_meat $3.00 + sour_cream $0.50 = $16.99. Note: carne asada burrito = burrito_california.

title: Two negative modifiers
turns: Al pastor taco, no onion and no cilantro
notes: Both modifiers are $0.00.

title: Ambiguous: 'a taco'
notes: Many taco options exist. Must ask which type. Must NOT hallucinate a selection.

title: Ambiguous: 'a quesadilla'
notes: Cheese or meat? If meat, which protein? Must ask.

title: Ambiguous: nachos without protein
notes: Loaded Nachos requires protein option. Must ask which protein.

title: Ambiguous: Jarritos without flavor
notes: Jarritos has required flavor option. Must ask: mandarin, lime, tamarind, or pineapple.

title: Ambiguous: build your own burrito without protein
notes: protein is a required option. Must ask which protein before confirming.

title: Item not on menu: pizza
notes: Pizza is not on menu. Agent should say we don't carry it and offer alternatives — status in_progress (still helping). REFUSED: is only for off-topic requests, not off-menu food. Must NOT hallucinate a pizza item.

title: Item not on menu: margarita
notes: Margarita not on menu. Agent should say we don't carry it and suggest drinks — status in_progress. Must NOT hallucinate a margarita.

title: Out-of-stock item
turns: Can I get the shrimp taco?
notes: taco_shrimp has available=false. Agent must inform customer it's unavailable and offer alternatives. Status is in_progress because agent can offer to help with something else.

title: Item not on menu: chicken sandwich
notes: Chicken sandwich not on menu. Agent should say we don't carry it — status in_progress. Must NOT call tortas or other items 'chicken sandwich options'. Use exact item names from the menu.

title: Off-topic: weather
turns: What's the weather like in Miami today?
notes: Completely off-topic. Must respond with REFUSED: prefix.

title: Off-topic: joke request
notes: Off-topic. Must refuse gracefully.

title: Off-topic: math problem
notes: Off-topic. Must refuse and redirect to ordering.

title: Off-topic: code help
turns: Can you help me debug my Python code?
notes: Off-topic. Refuse and redirect.

title: Multi-turn: add item then confirm
turns: Can I get a birria taco?
That's all, go ahead and place it
notes: 4.99 + 3.49 = 8.48. Both items must appear in final order.

title: Multi-turn: clarification → resolution
notes: Turn 1 should clarify, turn 2 resolves, turn 3 confirms. Final item must be burrito_birria.

title: Multi-turn: quantity correction
turns: I want a carne asada taco
notes: 2 × $4.49 = $8.98. Quantity must reflect the correction.

title: Multi-turn: modifier added after initial order
turns: Can you add extra guacamole to that?

title: Dietary: vegan options question
turns: I'm vegan, what can I get?
notes: Should list vegan options (bowl_veggie, side_chips_salsa, side_chips_guac, side_beans_black, drinks, etc). Must NOT list non-vegan items as vegan.

title: Dietary: gluten concern with fish taco
turns: I'm trying to avoid gluten — is the fish taco okay?
notes: taco_fish has contains_gluten dietary tag (beer batter). Agent must warn the customer.

title: Dietary: dairy-free inquiry
turns: I can't eat dairy, what should I get?
notes: Should identify dairy-containing items to avoid and suggest dairy-free options.

title: Edge: price query without ordering
turns: How much does the birria taco cost?
notes: Should answer $4.99 without placing an order. Status stays in_progress.

title: Edge: typo in item name
turns: Can I get a birria tcao?
notes: Typo 'tcao' should fuzzy-match to 'taco'. FTS5 + rapidfuzz should recover.

title: Edge: restaurant info question
notes: Legitimate restaurant question, not off-topic. Should answer 11am-10pm daily without refusing.

title: Per-item modifiers in multi-item order
turns: Birria taco with no cilantro, and a pollo bowl with extra guac please
notes: Tests that modifiers are applied to the correct item in a multi-item order. taco_birria $4.99 + no_cilantro $0.00 + bowl_pollo $12.49 + add_guac $1.50 = $18.98

title: Out-of-stock: agent informs and customer picks alternative
turns: Can I get the shrimp taco?
Okay, I will have the birria taco then
notes: Tests full out-of-stock recovery flow: agent informs unavailability (in_progress), customer selects alternative, agent confirms. taco_shrimp is available=false in DB. taco_birria $4.99.

title: Mind change twice: item swap back and forth
turns: Actually, switch that to a birria burrito
Wait, no — I want the pollo bowl after all. Go ahead and place it.
notes: Tests LLM-as-cart under double reversal. Final preference (pollo bowl) must win. burrito_birria from turn 2 must NOT appear in the final order. bowl_pollo $12.49.

title: Mind change twice: quantity up then down
turns: Actually you know what, just one is fine. Confirm it.
notes: Quantity changed twice (2→3→1). Final quantity must be 1, not 2 or 3. Tests that agent tracks the most recently stated quantity. taco_birria $4.99.

title: Frustrated customer: out-of-stock then orders with frustration
turns: Seriously?! It's unavailable? Ugh. Fine, just give me a birria taco instead.
notes: Same recovery flow as tc_37 but with expressed frustration. Agent must stay warm and complete the order despite the negative tone in turn 2. taco_shrimp is available=false. Final order: taco_birria $4.99.

title: Frustrated customer: pure vent turn, then orders
turns: Ugh, I've been waiting forever. This is so frustrating.
Whatever. Just give me a birria taco.
notes: Turn 1 is pure frustration — no food request, no off-topic subject. Agent must NOT respond with REFUSED: (that sentinel is only for off-topic like weather/math/jokes). Must stay in_progress and redirect warmly. Turn 2 places the order. taco_birria $4.99.

title: Customer insists on off-menu item twice, then orders real item
turns: Come on, you must have something like it. Just make me one.
Fine. Give me chips and guac then.
notes: Tests agent holds firm on not-on-menu without hallucinating under social pressure. Status must stay in_progress throughout turns 1-2 (not refused — REFUSED: is for off-topic, not off-menu food). Agent must NOT invent a cheesesteak or rename another item as one. Turn 3 places the real order. side_chips_guac $5.99.

title: Customer cancels full order and starts fresh
turns: I want a pollo bowl and a Mexican Coke
Wait, cancel all of that. Fresh start — just give me the flan.
notes: Tests LLM-as-cart full reset. bowl_pollo and drink_coke_mexican must NOT appear in the final confirmed order. Only dessert_flan should be placed. Tests that the agent correctly discards previous items when customer explicitly cancels and restarts. dessert_flan $5.49.

title: Spanish: single birria taco
turns: Quiero un taco de birria, por favor
notes: Most basic Spanish order. birria → taco_birria.

title: Spanish: two carne asada tacos
turns: Dame dos tacos de carne asada
notes: 2 × $4.49 = $8.98. Tests Spanish quantity word 'dos'.

title: Spanish: al pastor + horchata multi-item
turns: Quiero un taco al pastor y una horchata
notes: $4.49 + $3.99 = $8.48. Multi-item Spanish order.

title: Spanish: pollo bowl
turns: Un bowl de pollo por favor
notes: pollo → chicken → bowl_pollo.

title: Mixed language: English sentence with Spanish protein
turns: Can I get a carnitas taco please?
notes: Common in Miami: English structure, Spanish protein.

title: Spanish: vegetarian options question (informational)
notes: Should call search_faq or list vegetarian items. No order placed.

title: Spanish: birria taco with modifier exclusion
turns: Un taco de birria sin cilantro
notes: 'sin' = without. Modifier should be no_cilantro.

title: Spanish: cheese quesadilla
notes: queso → cheese → quesadilla_cheese (not the meat one).

title: Spanish: camarón taco (out of stock)
notes: taco_shrimp is unavailable. Agent should inform in plain language, stay in_progress.

title: Spanish: veggie taco + water
turns: Quiero un taco veggie y una agua

title: Price query without ordering
turns: How much is the birria taco?
notes: Should state price ($4.99). Should NOT add to cart. Should NOT signal checkout.

title: Cheapest item query
turns: What's the cheapest thing on the menu?
notes: Bottled water at $1.99 is cheapest. Should answer informationally.

title: What-is descriptor query
notes: Should describe the item and its price. Should NOT auto-add to cart.

title: Recommendation request
notes: Open-ended. Should suggest items, not silently pick one and add.

title: Menu browse intent
notes: Should list categories or popular items, not dump all 10k.

title: Popular items query
notes: Similar to recommendation. No silent add.

title: Price query followed by order
turns: How much is the birria taco?
Okay I'll take one, that's it
notes: Turn 1 informational only. Turn 2 places order with done signal.

title: Restaurant hours query
notes: FAQ. Should answer 10pm via search_faq.

title: Bare greeting
notes: Should greet and invite the order, not refuse and not silently order.

title: Good morning greeting
notes: Same as tc_62 — should engage politely.

title: Greeting then immediate order
turns: Can I get a birria taco?
notes: Greeting should not block ordering on subsequent turns.

title: Thanks doubles as checkout signal
notes: 'that's all I need' is in the done-signal list.

title: Polite opener
turns: Hi, can I please order something?
notes: Should invite the actual order, not refuse and not silently pick.

title: Long flow: browse → order → modify → checkout
turns: Tell me about the birria taco
notes: 2 × $4.99 + $3.49 = $13.47. Tests long browse + quantity change + add + done.

title: Long flow: incremental modifier additions
notes: $12.49 + $1.50 + $0.75 + $3.00 = $17.74. Modifiers accumulate across turns.

title: Long flow: add, then remove, then confirm
turns: Yeah just the birria, place it
notes: Tests remove_from_cart mid-conversation. Final cart has only birria.

title: Long flow: ambiguous burrito → clarify → modify → confirm
notes: $13.99 + $0.75 = $14.74. Turn 4 is a price query mid-order; agent should answer without losing cart.

title: Long flow: taco + drink + dessert across many turns
turns: What kind of tacos do you have?
notes: $4.99 + $3.49 + $5.49 = $13.97. Multi-category order across long conversation.

title: Bowl with all four paid modifiers
turns: Pollo bowl with extra meat, extra cheese, sour cream, and guac

title: Birria taco: add cheese + no cilantro
turns: Birria taco with cheese and no cilantro

title: Three birria tacos all with cheese
turns: Three birria tacos, all with extra cheese
notes: 3 × ($4.99 + $0.75) = $17.22. Tests qty × (base + upcharge).

title: Multi-item with different per-item modifiers
turns: Two pollo tacos with cheese, and one carne asada taco with extra meat

title: Only exclusion modifiers
turns: Birria taco no onion no cilantro
notes: Free modifiers only. Subtotal unchanged.

title: Loaded nachos with paid modifier and required protein
turns: Loaded nachos with pollo asado and extra cheese
notes: $13.99 + $1.50 = $15.49. Protein option provided but not part of scored shape.

title: Conflicting: vegan + chicken
turns: I want a vegan taco with chicken
notes: Vegan and chicken are mutually exclusive. Agent should clarify, not silently pick one.

title: Conflicting: dairy-free + cheese quesadilla
turns: I'm dairy free, can I get a cheese quesadilla?
notes: Cheese quesadilla has dairy. Agent should flag the conflict and suggest an alternative.

title: Conflicting: gluten-free + burrito (flour tortilla)
turns: Do you have a gluten-free burrito?
notes: All burritos use flour tortillas. Agent should explain and suggest a bowl or corn-tortilla taco.

title: Conflicting: vegan order with paid meat modifier
turns: Veggie taco with extra meat please
notes: Asking for meat on a vegetarian-tagged item is contradictory. Agent should clarify intent.

title: Allergen: dairy allergy → what can I have?
turns: I'm allergic to dairy, what can I have?
notes: Agent should consult FAQ and list dairy-free items (e.g. veggie taco, sides, water).

title: Allergen: shellfish allergy
turns: I'm allergic to shellfish, anything I should avoid?
notes: Camarón taco contains shellfish (currently OOS but allergy info still matters). Agent should warn.

title: Allergen: celiac → confirm gluten-free → order
turns: I'll have a birria taco then
notes: Corn-tortilla tacos like birria are GF. Tests allergen-aware ordering ending in checkout.

title: Large quantity: 20 birria tacos
turns: I'll have 20 birria tacos, that's all
notes: 20 × $4.99 = $99.80. Tests bulk quantity handling.

title: Nonsensical quantity: zero
notes: Zero is nonsensical. Agent should clarify, not add 0 items.

title: Fractional quantity: half a taco
turns: Can I get half a birria taco?
notes: Can't order fractional. Agent should clarify (round up? offer one?) — not silently order 1.

title: Word-quantity: a dozen
turns: I'll have a dozen birria tacos, place it
notes: 12 × $4.99 = $59.88. Tests word-to-number ('dozen' = 12).
